# Cheriven Empowerment Foundation (CEF)

> Cheriven Empowerment Foundation is a CAC-registered Nigerian non-profit, non-political and non-religious organisation (Incorporated Trustees, RC IT-145738) based in Abuja. It empowers vulnerable people — widows, women, youth and children — through education, vocational skills, sustainable livelihoods and social support.

## About

- Mission: To design, implement and scale inclusive empowerment programmes that improve access to education, vocational skills, sustainable livelihoods and social support for underserved and at-risk populations across Nigeria.
- Vision: A Nigerian society where vulnerable individuals — including widows, women, youth and children — are empowered to live with dignity, opportunity and hope.
- Values: compassion, sustainability, inclusion (including persons with special needs), and integrity.

## Programmes

- Education & Child Development — scholarships, school supplies and back-to-school support.
- Women & Widow Empowerment — grants, business training and support networks.
- Youth Skills & Employability — certified vocational training, entrepreneurship and mentorship.
- Vocational Training & Livelihoods — hands-on skills acquisition and graduate support.
- Social Support & Relief — food drives, healthcare outreach and emergency relief.
- Special Needs Inclusion — accessible, responsive programmes for persons with special needs.
